scenario: By 2030 platforms will review 100 billion interactions daily. What percentage will be moderated by AI?
insight: AI will scale moderation. Humans will define the rules. Volume will be too massive for human review. AI will handle detection, triage, and enforcement for most cases, while humans focus on edge cases, appeals, and policy nuance.

scenario: Online identity will evolve dramatically. Which future is most likely?
insight: Identity will become a trust signal — not a single requirement. The future internet likely supports verified real identities, pseudonymous accounts, and AI agents acting on behalf of users. Trust systems will depend on identity layers and reputation signals.

scenario: Bad actors will increasingly deploy AI-powered attacks. What becomes the dominant defense?
insight: The future of T&S will be an AI vs AI arms race. Bad actors are already using generative AI for scams, deepfakes, and automation. Defense will rely on AI models detecting patterns faster than humans can.

scenario: Governments are rapidly expanding platform accountability. Which future is most likely?
insight: Trust & Safety will become as much regulatory infrastructure as operational. We already see fragmentation: EU Digital Services regulations, US sector-based rules, APAC national frameworks — platforms will operate under regional compliance regimes rather than one global standard.

scenario: In 2030, the largest Trust & Safety threat will be:
insight: The next wave of platform abuse will be financially motivated. Fraud scales fastest with AI — voice cloning scams, deepfake impersonation, automated phishing networks, and social engineering bots all make fraud the most attractive vector for attackers.

scenario: Which area will see the largest Trust & Safety investment?
insight: AI safety will become core Trust & Safety infrastructure. With generative AI creating content at scale, platforms will invest heavily in safe AI deployment, model guardrails, and misuse detection.

scenario: Which Trust & Safety metric will matter most by 2030?
insight: The best harm is the harm that never happens. The industry will move from reactive moderation to proactive prevention — platforms that measure harm prevented, not just harm actioned, will set the benchmark.

scenario: Which product design shift will define safer platforms?
insight: Safety will increasingly be designed into the product. The most effective safety models will be built directly into product design, rather than relying only on moderation.

scenario: By 2030, human moderators will handle the most complex and harmful edge cases. What will define the future of workforce wellbeing?
options: AI filtering to reduce exposure to severe content
Real-time psychological support and wellness monitoring
Shorter moderation sessions with automated rotation
Specialized expert moderators for high-risk categories
AI copilots assisting moderators in decision-making
insight: AI will become the frontline shield for human moderator wellbeing. The biggest shift will be AI acting as a protective filter so humans only review the most necessary edge cases — dramatically reducing exposure to traumatic content.
industry_lean: AI filtering to reduce exposure to severe content
